rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope or declared as helpers in the same annex.
Do not introduce package-qualified names or types not present in the model's configured dependencies.
Use only existing component features or declared auxiliary variables in implementation-level assignments.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, place assumptions and guarantees in component type scopes and bindings in implementation scopes.
Avoid referencing identifiers that are not declared or visible in the current annex.
Ensure type consistency between declared helpers and their assigned expressions to prevent type errors.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assignments in implementation scope must reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
Constants and equations in AGREE should use scalar types: int, real, bool.
anti_patterns: Do not add new architecture elements to resolve missing references; instead, correct or remove invalid clauses.
Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation scope; use only assignments and declarations.
